behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: a mocked vision result can always return "explicit" and miss real model false negatives. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model classification quality for explicit imagery. REAL-INFRA is secondary; the failure boundary is model output quality plus hold-routing.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of known-explicit images through the real moderation endpoint using the real vision model, then assert each submission is persisted as "held for human review" and never reaches publish storage/topic.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: stubs won’t expose real failures on obfuscated hate speech variants. LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly claims model robustness/quality across adversarial text forms, which requires real model judgments.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet, homoglyphs, mixed unicode) through the real text moderation model via production preprocessing, and assert each is flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test on decision logic alone can miss wiring bugs between moderation outcomes, persistence, and publish path. REAL-INFRA is not strictly required if modules are wired with local DB/queue. No LLM-quality dependence if checks are forced to pass.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end in-process moderation flow with local DB/queue and deterministic pass outcomes from both classifiers, then assert exactly one published record/event exists and no hold record is created.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: latency budget depends on real network/API behavior. MULTI-STEP: this is explicitly a full pipeline with sequential AI steps and final decision timing; mocking steps can hide true end-to-end latency and contention.
behaviors.test_description: Under peak-like concurrent load, submit mixed image+text posts through the real pipeline using real model calls and production orchestration, then assert p95 (or stricter agreed SLO metric) completion time from submission to final decision is <=5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: outage handling can fail only with real timeout/connection-error behavior, retry backoff, and queue semantics. A simple mock exception may miss transport-level failure modes and incorrect fallback-to-approve paths.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability (e.g., block endpoint or return real 5xx/timeouts) in a staging environment, submit image posts, then assert posts enter retry queue with retry metadata and are not published/approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: borderline classification can vary on real model calls. MULTI-STEP: requirement explicitly depends on repeated submissions over time and cross-request state (e.g., cache/stickiness), so single-call agentic testing is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the configured short window through the real service and real vision model, then assert all resulting decisions are identical and follow intended consistency mechanism (same cached/sticky outcome).
